economy-and-jobs.summary: Signed major tax cuts in Arkansas and promotes deregulation to attract business investment.

healthcare-and-medicare.summary: Opposes Medicaid expansion and favors market-based healthcare solutions over government programs.

immigration-and-border-security.summary: Deployed Arkansas National Guard to the southern border and signed strict anti-illegal immigration laws.

education-and-student-debt.summary: Signed the LEARNS Act overhauling Arkansas education with school vouchers and eliminating teacher tenure.

national-defense-and-military.summary: Strongly supports increased military spending and a robust national defense posture.

climate-and-environment.summary: Opposes climate regulations and has rolled back environmental rules seen as burdensome to business.

criminal-justice-reform.summary: Favors tough-on-crime policies and has expanded law enforcement powers in Arkansas.

foreign-policy-and-diplomacy.summary: Supports a strong American presence abroad from her experience as White House press secretary.

technology-and-ai-regulation.summary: Generally opposes tech regulation but signed a law restricting minors' social media access.

social-security-and-medicare.summary: Supports maintaining Social Security benefits but favors reforms to reduce government spending overall.

gun-policy-and-2nd-amendment.summary: Signed permitless concealed carry and broadly opposes any new gun restrictions.

infrastructure-and-transportation.summary: Supports highway and broadband infrastructure investment for rural Arkansas communities.

housing-and-affordability.summary: Favors reducing regulations to spur housing construction rather than direct government subsidies.

energy-policy-and-oil-gas.summary: Strongly supports fossil fuel development and opposes federal restrictions on oil and gas production.

reproductive-rights.summary: Enforced Arkansas's near-total abortion ban with no exceptions for rape or incest.

lgbtq-rights.summary: Signed a ban on gender-affirming care for minors and restrictions on transgender athletes.

drug-policy.summary: Opposes marijuana legalization and supports strict drug enforcement policies.

voting-rights.summary: Signed voter ID requirements and opposes expanding mail-in voting access.

taxes-and-spending.summary: Signed significant income tax cuts and has aggressively pursued reducing state government spending.

labor-and-unions.summary: Supports Arkansas's right-to-work laws and opposes expanding union organizing protections.

privacy-and-surveillance.summary: Supports law enforcement surveillance tools but signed children's online privacy protections.

trade-and-tariffs.summary: Generally supports free trade but backed Trump-era tariff policies to protect American industries.

economy-and-jobs.summary: Signed California's $20 minimum wage for fast food workers and invests heavily in green economy jobs.

healthcare-and-medicare.summary: Expanded Medi-Cal to all income-eligible residents regardless of immigration status and pursues single-payer goals.

immigration-and-border-security.summary: Designated California as a sanctuary state and extended healthcare and driver's licenses to undocumented immigrants.

education-and-student-debt.summary: Invested record funding in California public schools and community colleges with free tuition programs.

national-defense-and-military.summary: Supports defense spending but emphasizes diplomacy and opposes unilateral military interventions.

climate-and-environment.summary: Signed executive order banning new gas car sales by 2035 and set the nation's most aggressive emissions targets.

criminal-justice-reform.summary: Imposed a moratorium on the death penalty and signed major policing and sentencing reform bills.

foreign-policy-and-diplomacy.summary: Engages in international climate diplomacy and favors multilateral cooperation on global challenges.

technology-and-ai-regulation.summary: Signed AI transparency and deepfake regulation bills while promoting California's tech sector growth.

social-security-and-medicare.summary: Strongly supports expanding Social Security and Medicare benefits and opposes any cuts to entitlements.

gun-policy-and-2nd-amendment.summary: Signed sweeping gun control laws including an assault weapons ban and proposed a 28th Amendment for gun safety.

infrastructure-and-transportation.summary: Championed California's high-speed rail project and massive investments in public transit and EV infrastructure.

housing-and-affordability.summary: Signed dozens of bills to increase housing density, streamline permitting, and fund affordable housing construction.

energy-policy-and-oil-gas.summary: Signed laws banning new oil wells near communities and aggressively transitioning California off fossil fuels.

reproductive-rights.summary: Enshrined abortion rights in California's constitution and made the state a sanctuary for reproductive healthcare.

lgbtq-rights.summary: Pioneer of same-sex marriage as San Francisco mayor and signed comprehensive LGBTQ+ protections as governor.

drug-policy.summary: Supports marijuana legalization and harm reduction approaches but vetoed a bill to create safe injection sites.

voting-rights.summary: Signed automatic voter registration, expanded mail-in voting, and same-day registration in California.

taxes-and-spending.summary: Supports progressive taxation and has maintained California's high tax rates to fund expansive social programs.

labor-and-unions.summary: Signed landmark fast-food worker protections and consistently supports union organizing and collective bargaining.

privacy-and-surveillance.summary: Signed the California Consumer Privacy Act amendments strengthening data privacy protections for residents.

trade-and-tariffs.summary: Favors free trade and international economic cooperation, particularly on climate-related trade agreements.

economy-and-jobs.summary: Focuses on growing Maine's workforce through job training programs and attracting businesses to rural areas.

healthcare-and-medicare.summary: Expanded Medicaid on her first day in office, covering 90,000 additional Mainers.

immigration-and-border-security.summary: Welcomes refugee resettlement in Maine and supports comprehensive immigration reform with border security.

education-and-student-debt.summary: Increased K-12 education funding and expanded access to community college and workforce training.

national-defense-and-military.summary: Supports Maine's military installations and the defense industry as key economic drivers for the state.

climate-and-environment.summary: Signed legislation committing Maine to 80% renewable energy by 2030 and carbon neutrality by 2045.

criminal-justice-reform.summary: As former AG, supports balanced reforms including diversion programs while maintaining public safety.

foreign-policy-and-diplomacy.summary: Supports diplomacy and international trade relationships that benefit Maine's fishing and forestry industries.

technology-and-ai-regulation.summary: Supports broadband expansion to rural Maine and measured regulation of emerging technologies.

social-security-and-medicare.summary: Strongly supports protecting Social Security and Medicare for Maine's aging population.

gun-policy-and-2nd-amendment.summary: Signed a yellow flag law after the Lewiston shooting but respects Maine's strong hunting and gun ownership culture.

infrastructure-and-transportation.summary: Invested heavily in broadband, roads, and bridges using federal infrastructure funds for rural Maine.

housing-and-affordability.summary: Signed housing bills to address Maine's shortage and invested in affordable housing construction.

energy-policy-and-oil-gas.summary: Promotes offshore wind energy and renewable transition while phasing down fossil fuel dependence.

reproductive-rights.summary: Signed legislation codifying and expanding abortion rights in Maine through the entire pregnancy.

lgbtq-rights.summary: Supports LGBTQ+ protections and signed an executive order protecting gender-affirming care access.

drug-policy.summary: Focuses on treatment and recovery for Maine's opioid crisis while supporting marijuana legalization.

voting-rights.summary: Supports Maine's ranked-choice voting system and accessible voting for all eligible citizens.

taxes-and-spending.summary: Supports moderate tax policy and responsible spending while investing in social services.

labor-and-unions.summary: Signed bills strengthening worker protections and supports collective bargaining rights for Maine workers.

privacy-and-surveillance.summary: Supports data privacy protections and has taken a measured approach to surveillance technology.

trade-and-tariffs.summary: Supports trade policies that benefit Maine's lobster, forestry, and agricultural export industries.

economy-and-jobs.summary: Campaigned on cutting business regulations and making Missouri more competitive for economic growth.

healthcare-and-medicare.summary: Skeptical of government-run healthcare expansion though Missouri voters approved Medicaid expansion.

immigration-and-border-security.summary: Supports strict border enforcement and opposes sanctuary city policies in Missouri.

education-and-student-debt.summary: Supports school choice and voucher programs while opposing federal student debt cancellation.

national-defense-and-military.summary: Strongly supports military readiness and Missouri's defense installations including Fort Leonard Wood.

climate-and-environment.summary: Opposes aggressive climate regulations and prioritizes economic growth over environmental mandates.

criminal-justice-reform.summary: Favors tough-on-crime approach and supports law enforcement with additional funding and protections.

foreign-policy-and-diplomacy.summary: Supports strong national defense and American leadership in global affairs.

technology-and-ai-regulation.summary: Favors a light-touch approach to tech regulation to avoid stifling innovation.

social-security-and-medicare.summary: Supports maintaining Social Security and Medicare while advocating for long-term fiscal sustainability.

gun-policy-and-2nd-amendment.summary: Strong Second Amendment defender who supports Missouri's permissive gun laws and opposes new restrictions.

infrastructure-and-transportation.summary: As former transportation commissioner, infrastructure investment is a signature priority.

housing-and-affordability.summary: Supports reducing regulations to encourage housing development rather than direct government subsidies.

energy-policy-and-oil-gas.summary: Supports all-of-the-above energy policy with emphasis on fossil fuels and energy independence.

reproductive-rights.summary: Supports Missouri's near-total abortion ban and opposes efforts to restore abortion access.

lgbtq-rights.summary: Opposes transgender athlete participation in women's sports and gender-affirming care for minors.

drug-policy.summary: Opposes recreational marijuana despite Missouri voters approving it and favors strict drug enforcement.

voting-rights.summary: Supports voter ID requirements and opposes expanding mail-in voting access.

taxes-and-spending.summary: Campaigned on cutting taxes and reducing government spending to grow Missouri's economy.

labor-and-unions.summary: Supports right-to-work policies and opposes expanding union organizing mandates.

privacy-and-surveillance.summary: Supports law enforcement tools and takes a conservative approach to new privacy regulations.

trade-and-tariffs.summary: Supports trade policies that benefit Missouri's agricultural exports and manufacturing sector.

economy-and-jobs.summary: Focuses on growing North Carolina's tech and clean energy sectors while supporting small businesses.

healthcare-and-medicare.summary: Supports Medicaid expansion in North Carolina and lowering prescription drug costs for families.

immigration-and-border-security.summary: Favors comprehensive immigration reform with a pathway to citizenship and reasonable border security.

education-and-student-debt.summary: Prioritizes increasing teacher pay and public school funding as a core campaign platform.

national-defense-and-military.summary: Supports North Carolina's significant military presence including Fort Liberty and Camp Lejeune.

climate-and-environment.summary: Supports clean energy transition and environmental protection while balancing economic development.

criminal-justice-reform.summary: As former AG, pursued criminal justice reforms including opioid crisis prosecution and consumer protection.

foreign-policy-and-diplomacy.summary: Supports international cooperation and trade relationships that benefit North Carolina's economy.

technology-and-ai-regulation.summary: Supports responsible tech regulation and growing the Research Triangle's innovation ecosystem.

social-security-and-medicare.summary: Supports protecting and strengthening Social Security and Medicare programs.

gun-policy-and-2nd-amendment.summary: Supports universal background checks and red flag laws while respecting Second Amendment rights.

infrastructure-and-transportation.summary: Supports expanding broadband access and investing in transportation infrastructure across North Carolina.

housing-and-affordability.summary: Supports increasing affordable housing supply and addressing rising costs in North Carolina's growing cities.

energy-policy-and-oil-gas.summary: Favors transitioning to clean energy and opposes offshore drilling along North Carolina's coast.

reproductive-rights.summary: Campaigned strongly on protecting abortion access and opposes North Carolina's 12-week ban.

lgbtq-rights.summary: Supports LGBTQ+ nondiscrimination protections and opposes anti-transgender legislation.

drug-policy.summary: Focuses on combating the opioid epidemic through enforcement and treatment while supporting decriminalization reforms.

voting-rights.summary: Fought voter suppression efforts as AG and supports expanding voting access for all North Carolinians.

taxes-and-spending.summary: Supports targeted tax increases on high earners to fund education and healthcare investments.

labor-and-unions.summary: Supports strengthening worker protections and raising the minimum wage in North Carolina.

privacy-and-surveillance.summary: Pursued data privacy enforcement actions as AG and supports consumer data protections.

trade-and-tariffs.summary: Supports free trade agreements that benefit North Carolina's agricultural and manufacturing exports.

economy-and-jobs.summary: Invested in diversifying New Mexico's economy beyond oil and gas with focus on film, tech, and renewable energy.

healthcare-and-medicare.summary: Created the nation's first state-funded childcare program and expanded Medicaid access statewide.

immigration-and-border-security.summary: Withdrew National Guard troops from the border and signed legislation supporting immigrant communities.

education-and-student-debt.summary: Signed landmark legislation making college tuition-free for all New Mexico residents regardless of income.

national-defense-and-military.summary: Supports New Mexico's national labs and military installations as critical national security assets.

climate-and-environment.summary: Signed the Energy Transition Act committing New Mexico to 100% carbon-free electricity by 2045.

criminal-justice-reform.summary: Signed criminal justice reform legislation and issued a controversial public health order on gun violence.

foreign-policy-and-diplomacy.summary: Supports diplomatic engagement and trade relationships, particularly with Mexico as a border state governor.

technology-and-ai-regulation.summary: Promotes New Mexico's national labs and tech sector while supporting thoughtful regulation of emerging technologies.

social-security-and-medicare.summary: Strongly supports expanding social safety net programs for New Mexico's vulnerable populations.

gun-policy-and-2nd-amendment.summary: Issued emergency public health order suspending concealed carry in Albuquerque and signed red flag legislation.

infrastructure-and-transportation.summary: Invested in broadband expansion and road improvements, particularly in tribal and rural communities.

housing-and-affordability.summary: Invested in affordable housing programs and homelessness prevention in New Mexico's urban areas.

energy-policy-and-oil-gas.summary: Balances New Mexico's heavy reliance on oil and gas revenue with aggressive clean energy transition goals.

reproductive-rights.summary: Signed legislation codifying abortion rights and made New Mexico a safe haven for out-of-state patients.

lgbtq-rights.summary: Signed comprehensive LGBTQ+ protections and banned conversion therapy for minors.

drug-policy.summary: Signed cannabis legalization and supports harm reduction and treatment-focused approaches to the opioid crisis.

voting-rights.summary: Signed same-day voter registration and expanded early voting access in New Mexico.

taxes-and-spending.summary: Supports progressive taxation and increased government spending on education and social programs.

labor-and-unions.summary: Signed legislation raising the minimum wage and strengthening collective bargaining rights.

privacy-and-surveillance.summary: Supports data privacy protections though her emergency gun order raised civil liberties concerns.

trade-and-tariffs.summary: Supports free trade particularly with Mexico, New Mexico's top trading partner.

economy-and-jobs.summary: Focuses on economic diversification beyond gaming and tourism to attract new industries to Nevada.

healthcare-and-medicare.summary: Favors market-based healthcare solutions and has resisted expanding government health programs.

immigration-and-border-security.summary: Supports stronger border security as a former law enforcement officer but takes a moderate tone on immigration.

education-and-student-debt.summary: Signed school choice legislation and supports expanding educational options for Nevada families.

national-defense-and-military.summary: Supports Nevada's military installations including Nellis Air Force Base and strong national defense.

climate-and-environment.summary: Skeptical of aggressive climate mandates but supports Nevada's solar energy development for economic benefit.

criminal-justice-reform.summary: As former Clark County sheriff, favors tough-on-crime policies and vetoed criminal justice reform bills.

foreign-policy-and-diplomacy.summary: Supports American leadership and strong international trade relationships benefiting Nevada's tourism economy.

technology-and-ai-regulation.summary: Favors a business-friendly approach with minimal tech regulation to attract companies to Nevada.

social-security-and-medicare.summary: Supports maintaining Social Security and Medicare for Nevada's large retiree population.

gun-policy-and-2nd-amendment.summary: Vetoed several gun control bills despite the 2017 Las Vegas shooting, defending Second Amendment rights.

infrastructure-and-transportation.summary: Supports infrastructure investment to manage Nevada's rapid population growth and water challenges.

housing-and-affordability.summary: Supports reducing regulations to increase housing supply in Las Vegas and Reno's tight markets.

energy-policy-and-oil-gas.summary: Supports an all-of-the-above energy strategy including Nevada's significant solar and geothermal resources.

reproductive-rights.summary: Accepts Nevada's existing abortion protections codified by voter referendum and does not seek to restrict them.

lgbtq-rights.summary: Has not prioritized LGBTQ+ legislation either way and generally takes a moderate stance on social issues.

drug-policy.summary: Supports Nevada's existing marijuana legalization but opposes further drug decriminalization efforts.

voting-rights.summary: Vetoed automatic voter registration bills and expressed concerns about election security.

taxes-and-spending.summary: Opposes tax increases and has pushed for budget restraint to keep Nevada's low-tax business environment.

labor-and-unions.summary: Has clashed with Nevada's powerful culinary union and vetoed pro-labor legislation.

privacy-and-surveillance.summary: As former sheriff, generally supports law enforcement surveillance capabilities over privacy restrictions.

trade-and-tariffs.summary: Supports free trade that benefits Nevada's tourism, mining, and logistics industries.

economy-and-jobs.summary: Focuses on low taxes and deregulation, attracting major corporate relocations to Tennessee.

healthcare-and-medicare.summary: Rejected Medicaid expansion and favors block grants and market-based alternatives to government healthcare.

immigration-and-border-security.summary: Signed strict anti-sanctuary city legislation and deployed Tennessee National Guard to the southern border.

education-and-student-debt.summary: Signed universal school voucher legislation and opposes federal student debt cancellation programs.

national-defense-and-military.summary: Strongly supports the military and Tennessee's defense industry including Oak Ridge National Laboratory.

climate-and-environment.summary: Opposes climate regulations and has not set state-level emissions reduction targets.

criminal-justice-reform.summary: Signed criminal justice reform legislation focused on reducing recidivism and supporting reentry programs.

foreign-policy-and-diplomacy.summary: Supports American leadership abroad and trade relationships benefiting Tennessee's auto manufacturing sector.

technology-and-ai-regulation.summary: Signed the ELVIS Act protecting artists from AI but generally opposes tech regulation.

social-security-and-medicare.summary: Supports maintaining current benefits while favoring long-term reforms to reduce entitlement spending.

gun-policy-and-2nd-amendment.summary: Signed permitless carry despite the Covenant School shooting and strongly opposes new gun restrictions.

infrastructure-and-transportation.summary: Proposed a major transportation plan including a new choice lanes highway program for Tennessee.

housing-and-affordability.summary: Supports market-based approaches to housing affordability as Nashville and Tennessee cities grow rapidly.

energy-policy-and-oil-gas.summary: Supports all fossil fuel development and opposes federal energy transition mandates.

reproductive-rights.summary: Signed Tennessee's trigger law banning nearly all abortions with very limited medical exceptions.

lgbtq-rights.summary: Signed bans on gender-affirming care for minors, drag performances near children, and transgender athlete restrictions.

drug-policy.summary: Opposes marijuana legalization and maintains strict drug enforcement in Tennessee.

voting-rights.summary: Signed legislation criminalizing some voter registration activities and supports strict voter ID requirements.

taxes-and-spending.summary: Maintains Tennessee's no-income-tax status and pursues conservative fiscal policies.

labor-and-unions.summary: Supports Tennessee's right-to-work status and opposed the UAW unionization effort at Volkswagen.

privacy-and-surveillance.summary: Generally supports law enforcement tools but signed limited data privacy protections for minors.

trade-and-tariffs.summary: Supports trade policies benefiting Tennessee's massive auto manufacturing and agricultural export sectors.

economy-and-jobs.summary: Focuses on lowering costs for Virginia families and supporting small businesses and the defense sector workforce.

healthcare-and-medicare.summary: Championed legislation to lower prescription drug prices and supports expanding affordable healthcare access.

immigration-and-border-security.summary: Supports comprehensive immigration reform with both a pathway to citizenship and enhanced border security.

education-and-student-debt.summary: Supports increased public education funding and targeted student debt relief for public service workers.

national-defense-and-military.summary: Former CIA officer who strongly supports defense spending and Virginia's intelligence and military communities.

climate-and-environment.summary: Supports clean energy investment and Chesapeake Bay conservation while balancing economic impacts.

criminal-justice-reform.summary: Supports targeted policing reforms and community safety programs with a law enforcement background perspective.

foreign-policy-and-diplomacy.summary: As a former CIA case officer, strongly supports diplomacy, intelligence capabilities, and international alliances.

technology-and-ai-regulation.summary: Supports responsible AI regulation informed by her intelligence background and Northern Virginia's tech hub.

social-security-and-medicare.summary: Strongly opposes cuts to Social Security and Medicare and supports strengthening these programs.

gun-policy-and-2nd-amendment.summary: Supports universal background checks and red flag laws while respecting responsible gun ownership.

infrastructure-and-transportation.summary: Supports infrastructure investment including broadband for rural Virginia and reducing Northern Virginia traffic congestion.

housing-and-affordability.summary: Supports increasing housing supply and affordability programs for Virginia's growing communities.

energy-policy-and-oil-gas.summary: Supports transitioning to clean energy while maintaining energy reliability during the transition period.

reproductive-rights.summary: Strongly supports protecting abortion access and opposes Virginia's proposed 15-week ban.

lgbtq-rights.summary: Supports LGBTQ+ nondiscrimination protections and the Equality Act.

drug-policy.summary: Supports marijuana decriminalization and treatment-focused approaches to substance abuse.

voting-rights.summary: Supports expanding voting access including early voting and opposes voter suppression efforts.

taxes-and-spending.summary: Takes a fiscally moderate approach supporting targeted investments while emphasizing deficit reduction.

labor-and-unions.summary: Supports worker protections and collective bargaining rights for Virginia's public and private sector workers.

privacy-and-surveillance.summary: Supports privacy protections informed by her intelligence background, balancing security with civil liberties.

trade-and-tariffs.summary: Supports free trade agreements that benefit Virginia's agricultural sector and Port of Virginia.

economy-and-jobs.summary: Focuses on rebuilding the USVI economy after hurricanes through tourism development and infrastructure investment.

healthcare-and-medicare.summary: Advocates for equitable Medicaid funding for territories and rebuilding the USVI healthcare system.

immigration-and-border-security.summary: Supports immigration reform that recognizes the unique needs of US territories in the Caribbean.

education-and-student-debt.summary: Invested in rebuilding USVI schools after hurricanes and expanding University of the Virgin Islands programs.

national-defense-and-military.summary: Supports federal military presence in the Caribbean and disaster preparedness for the territory.

climate-and-environment.summary: Committed to transitioning USVI to 100% clean energy by 2050 given the territory's hurricane vulnerability.

criminal-justice-reform.summary: Supports reforming the USVI justice system with focus on rehabilitation and reducing violent crime.

foreign-policy-and-diplomacy.summary: Advocates for stronger US-Caribbean diplomatic relationships that benefit the Virgin Islands.

technology-and-ai-regulation.summary: Supports technology investment and broadband expansion to modernize the USVI economy.

social-security-and-medicare.summary: Fights for equitable Social Security and Medicare treatment for US territory residents.

gun-policy-and-2nd-amendment.summary: Supports gun safety measures given high violent crime rates in the US Virgin Islands.

infrastructure-and-transportation.summary: Top priority is rebuilding USVI infrastructure devastated by Hurricanes Irma and Maria.

housing-and-affordability.summary: Invested in rebuilding and constructing affordable housing following devastating hurricane damage.

energy-policy-and-oil-gas.summary: Supports transitioning away from expensive imported fossil fuels to solar and wind energy in the USVI.

reproductive-rights.summary: Supports maintaining access to reproductive healthcare services in the US Virgin Islands.

lgbtq-rights.summary: Supports LGBTQ+ protections and nondiscrimination policies in the territory.

drug-policy.summary: Signed legislation to legalize and regulate adult-use marijuana in the US Virgin Islands.

voting-rights.summary: Advocates for full voting representation for US territories including congressional representation.

taxes-and-spending.summary: Supports federal investment in territories and maintains tax incentive programs to attract business to USVI.

labor-and-unions.summary: Supports worker protections and fair wages for USVI's tourism and service industry workforce.

privacy-and-surveillance.summary: Has not prioritized privacy or surveillance legislation in the territory.

trade-and-tariffs.summary: Supports trade policies that reduce costs for the USVI given the territory's reliance on imported goods.
